title: Quorum Queues and Replicated Delivery Claims · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Quorum Use Case Fit
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 9561 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to compute majority availability while separating quorum custody from stakeholder effect; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Quorum Use Case Fit.

For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Quorum Use Case Fit, keep Quorum Use Case Fit connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.
